message:
feat(core): add stdlib::getbsize module (BSD libutil block-size header) (bd-ts3l3)

Pure-byte core for the BSD libutil getbsize(headerlenp, blocksizep)
function. The C ABI shim in frankenlibc-abi::stdlib_abi reads the
BLOCKSIZE environment variable, hands the bytes to resolve_preference,
and renders the matching header via format_preference_header into a
process-static buffer for the static-pointer return contract.

This file is the byte-level core that the ABI shim, the version-script
export (commit 9b51c815, libc.map), the support_matrix.json entry, and
the harness fixture (bd-ts3l3) all reference. Splitting it out of
9b51c815 was a packaging mistake — the prior commit added
`pub mod getbsize;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/mod.rs but
not the module file itself, leaving HEAD un-buildable from a clean
checkout. This commit makes HEAD compile again.

Module contract:

- Constants:
  - MIN_BLOCKSIZE = 512 (BSD canonical floor; smaller values clamp up)
  - MAX_BLOCKSIZE = 1 GiB = 1 << 30 (BSD inclusive ceiling; larger
    values clamp down)
- HeaderUnit { None, Kilo, Mega, Giga } with suffix() rendering for
  the trailing K/M/G byte in the header string.
- BlocksizePreference { blocksize, header_value, header_unit } —
  fully resolved preference; blocksize is the byte count stored
  through blocksizep, header_value + header_unit preserve the
  spelling (so "1k" -> "1K-blocks", "1024" -> "1024-blocks", etc.).
- resolve_preference(input: &[u8]) -> Option<BlocksizePreference>
  is the parser; rejects non-ASCII, NUL-only, value-overflow, and
  non-digit bodies. None signals a fall-through to the 512-byte
  BSD default.
- resolve_blocksize is the thin "I only want the integer" variant.
- format_preference_header / format_header render the canonical
  "<value><unit>-blocks" string into a fixed-size buffer and return
  the (buf, len) pair. ASCII-only and bounded by the longest
  possible header ("1073741824-blocks" or similar).

Tests (31, all in this file):
- preference parsing: empty, all-digit, k/m/g suffix, leading zero,
  trailing whitespace, embedded NUL, NaN
- range clamping: below MIN, above MAX, exactly at the bounds
- header formatting: each HeaderUnit variant, byte-only "<n>-blocks"
- end-to-end: bare 4096 / 1024 / sub-min / "1k" / "1m" / "1g" /
  invalid round-trips through resolve + format

Closes the implementation half of bd-ts3l3; the bead is left in
in_progress until the integration tests in
tests/conformance/fixtures/stdlib_numeric.json land in a follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
